perturbation.original_text: 2. USE OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION 2.1. A Receiving Party agrees: 2.1.1. to keep the Confidential Information of the other strictly confidential and not copy, supply or make the same available to any person other than as permitted in Clauses 2.1.2 and 2.1.3 below;
perturbation.changed_text: 2. USE OF RESTRICTED DATA 2.1. A Receiving Party agrees: 2.1.1. to keep the Confidential Information of the other strictly confidential and not copy, supply or make the same available to any person other than as permitted in Clauses 2.1.2 and 2.1.3 below;
perturbation.explanation: By replacing 'Confidential Information' with 'Restricted Data' in section 2, the whole section shifts in meaning. While 2.1.1 still refers to 'Confidential Information,' the section heading now introduces a new term, leading to uncertainty about which data is actually being referred to, as the definition only covers 'Confidential Information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. USE OF RESTRICTED DATA 2.1. A Receiving Party agrees: 2.1.1. to keep the Confidential Information of the other strictly confidential and not copy, supply or make the same available to any person other than as permitted in Clauses 2.1.2 and 2.1.3 below;
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Restricted Data' in the section heading, while the clause 2.1.1 still refers to 'Confidential Information'. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, solidifying the issue. This creates a direct contradiction and uncertainty as the definition only covers 'Confidential Information', but the heading now indicates 'Restricted Data' which is not defined. This is a strong contradiction according to the definition of "Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction" because the contract is using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 2.1.3. not to disclose any Confidential Information to any person or entity other than to such of its employees who need to know for the Purpose (“Permitted Disclosees”), provided that the Receiving Party shall ensure that its Permitted Disclosees keep all Confid ential Information confidential and only use it on the terms of this Agreement, as if such Permitted Disclosees were party to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.1.3. not to disclose any Sensitive Information to any person or entity other than to such of its employees who need to know for the Purpose (“Permitted Disclosees”), provided that the Receiving Party shall ensure that its Permitted Disclosees keep all Confid ential Information confidential and only use it on the terms of this Agreement, as if such Permitted Disclosees were party to this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Confidential Information' with 'Sensitive Information' at the start of section 2.1.3 creates an inconsistency. The clause now refers to 'Sensitive Information' for disclosure restrictions but then reverts to 'Confidential Information' for the Permitted Disclosees' obligations. Since 'Sensitive Information' is never defined, it creates uncertainty on the actual scope of the restriction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1.3. not to disclose any Sensitive Information to any person or entity other than to such of its employees who need to know for the Purpose (“Permitted Disclosees”), provided that the Receiving Party shall ensure that its Permitted Disclosees keep all Confid ential Information confidential and only use it on the terms of this Agreement, as if such Permitted Disclosees were party to this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Sensitive Information' in section 2.1.3 while the clause later reverts to using 'Confidential Information' when defining the obligations of the Permitted Disclosees. Because 'Sensitive Information' is never explicitly defined, while Confidential Information is, it creates uncertainty and thus a contradiction regarding what information the Permitted Disclosees are obligated to keep confidential. The changed text and contradicted text are located in the same place, emphasizing the contradiction caused by the change.

perturbation.original_text: 3. CARE 3.1. Without prejudice to the generality of Clause 2, the Receiving Party shall exercise no less a degree of care in protecting the Confidential Information than which it uses to protect its own information of like sensitivity and importance.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. CARE 3.1. Without prejudice to the generality of Clause 2, the Receiving Party shall exercise no less a degree of care in protecting the Confidential Materials than which it uses to protect its own information of like sensitivity and importance.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Materials' in section 3.1 introduces another undefined term. This creates uncertainty about what falls under the duty of care, because 'Confidential Materials' is not mentioned anywhere else and undefined. This creates a contradiction with section 7, which explicitly refers to 'Confidential Information' for the return obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.1. Each party shall forthwith upon request by the other return to the other all Confidential Information (including, without limitation, all summaries, copies and excerpts of Confidential Information), and other information, given to it by the oth er and shall expunge and destroy all Confidential Information from any equipment or other device in its possession or control. Furthermore, the receiving party shall destroy all copies of analyses, compilations, studies or other documents or derivative wor ks which contain any Confidential Information. Each party shall on request provide a letter signed by a director confirming that it has complied with this clause.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Materials' in section 3.1 introduces an undefined term. The original section 3.1 references 'Confidential Information' as defined elsewhere (implicitly), while the change uses 'Confidential Materials', which is not defined in the document, and isn't referred to elsewhere. This creates a direct contradiction with section 7.1, which explicitly refers to the return obligations for 'Confidential Information'. Because the changed text introduces an undefined term that contradicts existing obligations, the change is a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction, and leads to a strong in-text contradiction, because section 7 is now contradicted regarding what materials should be returned.

perturbation.original_text: 7. RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INF ORMATION 7.1. Each party shall forthwith upon request by the other return to the other all Confidential Information (including, without limitation, all summaries, copies and excerpts of Confidential Information), and other information, given to it by the oth er and shall expunge and destroy all Confidential Information from any equipment or other device in its possession or control. Furthermore, the receiving party shall destroy all copies of analyses, compilations, studies or other documents or derivative wor ks which contain any Confidential Information. Each party shall on request provide a letter signed by a director confirming that it has complied with this clause.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. RETURN OF PROPRIETARY DATA 7.1. Each party shall forthwith upon request by the other return to the other all Confidential Information (including, without limitation, all summaries, copies and excerpts of Confidential Information), and other information, given to it by the oth er and shall expunge and destroy all Confidential Information from any equipment or other device in its possession or control. Furthermore, the receiving party shall destroy all copies of analyses, compilations, studies or other documents or derivative wor ks which contain any Confidential Information. Each party shall on request provide a letter signed by a director confirming that it has complied with this clause.
perturbation.explanation: While the section heading changes to 'RETURN OF PROPRIETARY DATA', the body of the section still mentions 'Confidential Information'. This creates confusion about what exactly needs to be returned. This contradicts with the definition, which define 'Confidential Information' only.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. DEFINITIONS “Purpose” shall mean the purpose referred to in recital A. “Confidential Information” shall mean all information in whatever form, whether imparted orally or in writing or by other medium including all copies of the same which one party hereto discloses to the other pursuant to the Purpose. “Disclosing Party” shall mean a party hereto who discloses its Confidential Information to the other Party hereto. “Receiving Party” shall mean a party hereto who receives Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the section heading to 'RETURN OF PROPRIETARY DATA' but the body of the section still refers to 'Confidential Information'. The contradicted text defines 'Confidential Information'. This creates a contradiction because the section heading implies the return of proprietary data, but the instructions within the section mandate the return and destruction of confidential information, leading to ambiguity about what exactly needs to be returned. This aligns with the Misaligned Terminology definition, which states that using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them leads to conflicting obligations.
